resources.string: Press again to exit AnyBase Kernel
Read this agreement completely. By tapping Agree and continue, you confirm that you understand and accept all terms. If you do not agree, tap Disagree and exit.

ABK triggers GitHub Actions builds, downloads, flashes, or installs GKI KernelSU / SUSFS artifacts, and provides root checks, GitHub authorization, fork checks, custom external module injection, build progress sync, and artifact management.

ABK is intended for learning, research, personal use, and debugging on legally authorized devices. It does not guarantee compatibility, boot success, flash success, root availability, detection bypass, or long-term stability.

Building, modifying, flashing boot, init_boot, vendor_boot, kernel images, AnyKernel3 packages, or installing low-level modules are high-risk operations and may cause boot failure, reboot loops, data corruption, partition issues, system service failure, or warranty/service limits.
If failures occur, you may need to restore official images, reflash firmware, wipe data, unlock or relock the bootloader. You are responsible for all costs and consequences.
Do not continue building or flashing if you are unsure about the device model, partition layout, Android version, kernel version, security patch level, KMI compatibility, or rollback plan.
Use ABK only on devices, accounts, repositories, and networks that you own or are explicitly authorized to use.

Do not use ABK, workflows, patches, custom external modules, or build artifacts for unauthorized access, risk-control bypass, cheating, malicious hiding, data theft, service disruption, audit evasion, bulk abuse, infringement, or any illegal use.
You are responsible for confirming local laws, platform rules, device vendor terms, and upstream project license requirements.

ABK integrates multiple third-party projects, patches, scripts, and download sources. Third-party code, licenses, stability, security, and compatibility remain the responsibility of their upstream projects.
Enabling a custom external module clones an external repository and runs setup.sh from its root. Review script contents, commit history, source trust, and permission impact before execution.

External modules may modify kernel source, defconfig, build scripts, or artifact contents. Build failures, device issues, security risks, or compliance issues caused by external modules are the responsibility of the user and module provider.

ABK uses GitHub Device Flow to obtain an authorization token for fork checks or management, Actions triggers, build status reads, and artifact downloads. Understand the authorization scope and manage your GitHub account security.
ABK may request root when flashing, installing modules, or identifying local state. Granting root increases system risk. Confirm that the operation is necessary.
Do not expose tokens, keys, private data, sensitive device information, or non-public build artifacts in custom modules, public repositories, logs, or issues.

To the extent allowed by law, ABK developers, maintainers, and contributors are not liable for device damage, data loss, account risk, service interruption, compliance issues, third-party claims, or any direct or indirect loss caused by using, modifying, distributing, or relying on ABK or its build artifacts.
ABK is provided as is. It does not promise defect-free, uninterrupted, or risk-free operation, nor availability for any specific device, system version, kernel branch, or third-party module.
Continuing to use ABK means you have the required knowledge, backup, and recovery ability, and that you accept all risks.
Read to the end of this agreement before tapping Agree and continue.
ABK requires root permission to work properly. Grant permission and try again.
Root is used only when flashing, installing modules, or checking root-related capabilities. Kernel version detection does not require root.
After authorization, GitHub sign-in and fork checks start automatically.
ABK needs GitHub authorization to manage the fork repository and trigger kernel build workflows.
Open the following address in a browser and enter the verification code to authorize:
ABK will request repo and workflow permissions to check/sync your fork and trigger kernel build workflows.
No fork of this repository was detected. ABK needs a fork before it can trigger workflows.
ABK is verifying your repository, workflow, and upstream sync state.
Your build repository will be created
Your fork is %1$d commits behind upstream. Sync before building is recommended.
Fork, permissions, and workflow state passed checks.
Your fork is behind upstream. Sync it to get the latest build configuration.
This is shown only on first launch. You can skip now and sign in to GitHub later when you actually need builds.
After signing in, ABK can check your fork, trigger workflows, and sync build status.
If you do not need to build right now, skip for now. The Build tab can resume the GitHub sign-in and fork flow later.
The Flash tab can still browse prebuilt GKI
When signed out, the Flash tab keeps only prebuilt GKI downloads from Releases.
Workflow artifacts, fork repository access, and Actions records return after sign-in and fork checks complete.
Sign in to GitHub before building
ABK no longer forces GitHub sign-in at startup. Enter OOBE here when you are ready to build.
This account is already signed in, but there is no usable fork yet. Continue OOBE so ABK can check and finish the fork setup.
Choose the standard GKI build or a OnePlus/Oplus device build.
A build is active, so this configuration will be added to the local queue and dispatched in order.
Before submitting a build, ABK can not confirm or enable the GitHub Actions build workflow.
Sign in to GitHub in the browser and confirm this account has Actions/Workflow permission for the fork repository.
After the page opens, manually enable the workflow if you see Enable workflow, then return to ABK and submit the build again.
ABK shows the system version, KMI, and CPU directly; manifest suffixes are only upstream XML names, not selection rules.

Builds the kernel from the commit of the latest successful upstream build-manager run on main. The manager APK is fetched via nightly.link from that same run. If the manager download fails, the kernel build still continues.
Do not inject any root authorization scheme. KSU branch, SUSFS, and KPM will be disabled automatically.
Build a rootless OnePlus kernel. SUSFS and KPM will be disabled automatically.
OnePlus builds use the upstream default branch for the selected KernelSU variant.
Proxy optimization is forced off for MTK branches.
The current OnePlus KMI has no available SUSFS upstream branch. Only android14/6.1 and android15/6.6 are supported; android12/5.10 and android13/5.15 are disabled automatically.
Queue config applied. You can continue editing.
Plan applied. You can continue editing.
Save, share, or import the current build configuration.
Paste a plan code starting with ABKP2:
Full plans include Android, kernel version, patch level, and feature settings. Feature-only plans keep the current page kernel version data when imported.
Save the current build configuration as a plan first.
Saved plans can be applied, shared, renamed, or deleted here.
Build submissions enter the queue automatically.
Submitting again while a build is active will place it here.
Queue items keep full build configs. Later page edits do not affect already queued items.
Delete \"%1$s\"? This will not affect the current build configuration.
Triggers GitHub Actions and automatically organizes img, AK3, manager, and SUSFS modules.
Builds a device-specific AnyKernel3 package from the OnePlus/Oplus manifest.
Preview: empty uses the workflow default local version
Preview: use current UTC at workflow runtime (example: %1$s)
%1$d more steps are tracked in the background
Match the Android version, KMI, and security patch date to the current system; use the device recommendation when unsure.
Choose the kernel permission solution and matching branch.
Enable only the module capabilities you need to keep debugging simpler.
Add extra kernel support for memory compression algorithms.
Run setup.sh from the external repository root by stage.
These fields are saved and will not reset next time.
Flashing a kernel is risky. Make sure you understand the consequences. Continue?
Partial activation only allows viewing downloaded files on the Files page.
Grant root permission before directly installing the manager app.
Grant root permission before flashing or installing modules.
Waiting for root shell. Do not leave the app...
Automatic flashing is not supported for this file type
Delete the local file record and downloaded file:\n%1$s
Downloaded local prebuilt GKI files will be deleted.
Cached artifact records for this workflow and downloaded local files will be deleted.\n\nWorkflow %1$s
Also delete remote GitHub Actions workflow record
After a successful build, ABK syncs online and organizes artifacts by workflow.
After a successful build, files can be downloaded and viewed here.
No browsable releases were found in this repository.
When signed out, the Flash tab keeps only prebuilt GKI downloads. Re-enable this feature in Settings.
This workflow artifact was refreshed or deleted.
This release has no GKI, boot, img, or AK3 assets matching the filters.
This release has no recognized prebuilt GKI assets.
This prebuilt GKI release was refreshed or deleted.
Select a workflow, then handle kernels, managers, and modules.
Partial activation only provides artifact downloads and file viewing.
No parseable parameter matrix in release content
%1$d releases · filter assets in the detail page
Only show assets matching current filters
Review artifact download progress or stop the current automatic download.
Waiting for automatic download to start
After the next build finishes, available artifacts will be organized here automatically.
Notification and in-app progress stay in sync
%1$d workflows are queued; merged progress is waiting for runner…
Used to generate default build parameters and confirm workflow source.
Quickly check the latest 5 workflow results.
%1$d workflows are running in parallel. Recent build records and the queue page can be viewed or cancelled separately.
Sign out of the GitHub account? You will need to authorize again.
Applies only to the next newly submitted build. Turning it off will not auto-download.
Fetch prebuilt GKI from this repository release. Downloads are manual.
Use Material 3 Expressive page back animation, independent from system predictive back.
Repository entry points, version information, and contributors
Licenses for repository references, embedded source, and dependencies
Use Material You dynamic colors generated from the system wallpaper
Available on Android 12 and later. Custom palette is used now.
Primary actions, selected states, and main accents
Secondary states, labels, and supporting accents
Select an image to enable a global background
Choose a local image as the app background
Restore the solid Material theme background
Lower values let cards, top bars, and bottom bars reveal the background.
AnyBase Kernel is an automation repository and Android app for building, distributing, and managing GKI KernelSU / SUSFS kernels.
Sorted alphabetically by GitHub username. Only identifiable contributor accounts are shown, with automation accounts filtered out.
This list is derived from repository files, workflows, the Gradle version catalog, and the npm lockfile. Upstream project licenses remain authoritative.
Embedded source, binaries, and download sources
ABK continues development on top of the following projects, repositories, and community work.
Manage build automation, notifications, theme, and repository sources.
GitHub account, fork repository, and sign out.
Sync workflow status to system notifications.
Control back gesture and page transition behavior.
Control app light and dark display.
Choose system dynamic colors or custom palette.
Theme and accent colors used when Monet is off.
Choose a background image and adjust upper UI transparency.
Load dynamically from current %1$s backend capabilities.
Load dynamically from current KSU backend capabilities.
Tools for SELinux and module umount paths.
Export or import the current root authorization list.
Result of the most recent tool operation.
Manage templates saved in ReSukiSU profile storage.
Result of the most recent template operation.
Edit App Profile template JSON directly.
Build artifacts, prebuilt GKI files, and modules are saved to this filesystem folder.
The selected folder cannot be converted to a filesystem path. Enter an absolute path or choose the local Download folder.
Download directory is not a folder: %1$s
Leave empty to connect directly to GitHub. If set, artifacts mirror to releases before download.
Waiting for root shell. Do not leave the app...
Operation incomplete. Refresh and try again.
Current kernel did not report ABK external modules
Install and enable a supported manager kernel to view runtime information
Build identity and current runtime backend
Build record written by the compiler
Current device output uses an old schema and does not include build parameters.
Some devices route module zip selection to a vendor security picker, which may not return the real file.
After all-files access is granted, ABK will continue opening the module picker. You can also keep using the system file picker without granting it.
After confirmation, ABK will call the module installer available on the current system. A reboot is usually required after installation.
This marks the standard module for removal. KernelSU will delete it after reboot.
This removes the pending uninstall flag and keeps the module installed.
Manage kernel permission profiles for other apps
Modules appear after refreshing a central repository
Add index repositories that contain abk-modules.json.
After adding a central repository, modules are merged into the main page.
Deleting a central repository does not remove single-module repositories already added to the build configuration.
Manage local and online App Profile templates
Loading available features from the current KSU backend
Choose a location to export the allowlist
Choose a backup file to import
Create a new App Profile template JSON
Recommended: select the init_boot partition image
Automatically detect the current boot / init_boot and patch directly
Patch and write to the other slot
Flash an AnyKernel3-format kernel zip package
Choose whether to flash the current slot or the other slot
Target partition for the current slot
Select a local .ko file or use bundled LKM
Advanced parameters are passed directly to the ksud bundled in this APK. Compatibility probing is not performed.
Always allow adb shell to call su. Enable only when needed.
Force USB debugging and disable adb authentication. Enable only when needed.
No bundled LKM matches the current variant and KMI. Select a local .ko file.
This APK does not include an executable bundled SukiSU-Ultra ksud, so it can not patch boot.img without root. Use an APK with bundled ksud, or grant root and continue.
Only AnyKernel3 zip files are supported
Unable to read the selected file
This module does not support uninstall
Finish GitHub sign-in and fork setup before submitting a build.
Workflow is still not enabled. Current state: %1$s
Build was submitted, but no workflow run was found yet. Refresh recent builds later.
Build was submitted, but no workflow run was found yet
Found a running workflow, waiting for runner
Failed to fetch prebuilt GKI releases: %1$s
Sign in before downloading build artifacts
No build artifact can be mirrored: %1$s
Mirror workflow does not exist. Sync your fork: %1$s
Mirror workflow was triggered, but no run record was found yet
Mirror release was created, but artifact was not found: %1$s.zip
Timed out waiting for mirror workflow
Unable to delete remote workflow record: repository information is incomplete
Failed to delete remote workflow record: %1$s
Template name can not be empty
Let the kernel unmount modules for apps that need it
Prevent apps from detecting SELinux changes
Run the adbd daemon as root
Record root-related events to the KernelSU superuser access log file
Global default for module unmount in App Profile
Global default for \"Unmount modules\" in App Profile
Available after ABK is recognized as the native manager
Useful for debugging WebUI. Enable only when needed.
The current backend is active, but ABK can not reliably identify its type. Unsafe setting injection was skipped.
The current %1$s backend does not support this feature
This feature is managed by the module and can not be changed directly in the manager
Legacy ABKP1 plan codes are too long and are no longer supported. Share the plan again.
External module count exceeds the limit
Negative numbers can not be written to a plan code
Plan code numeric field is invalid
Module repository URL can not be empty
No parseable build information summary was found in the job logs
No parseable build information summary was found in the workflow logs
Log reading is not complete yet
ABK has not been granted root permission, so it can not read the external root backend. Grant root to ABK first, or use a kernel that recognizes ABK as the native manager.
Only the external root / ksud compatibility layer is connected. ABK does not have native management permission and can not access the native bridge, manager settings, or root authorization policy.
ABK is connected to the kernel interface, but does not have native management permission. Confirm that the kernel recognizes the current ABK APK as the manager.
[ABK] Root shell unavailable; falling back to APK-bundled SukiSU-Ultra ksud to patch the local boot image only
No runnable APK-bundled SukiSU-Ultra ksud found; without root you can only generate a patched image after selecting boot.img.
This installation method requires root permission.
Only a generic su shell was detected; no KernelSU/ReSukiSU control interface usable for ABK runtime management was found.
Failed to toggle legacy su command support
No usable KernelSU/ReSukiSU manager interface or root shell was detected.
Currently working only through the ksud/root shell compatibility layer. ABK is not yet recognized by the kernel as the native manager and cannot manage root authorization policies.
Only a generic su shell is available; no KernelSU/ReSukiSU native manager interface was detected.

The KernelSU/ReSukiSU native interface is reachable, but the current ABK APK is not recognized as the manager. Make sure the installed app is the officially signed com.abk.kernel APK whose signature matches ABK_MANAGER_CERT_SHA256 used at kernel build time.
ABK control did not respond; the kernel may not have CONFIG_ABK_CONTROL enabled, or the ABK Control external module is missing the before_build stage.
[ABK] Invoking bundled libksud.so via root shell
[ABK] Using APK-bundled SukiSU-Ultra ksud for local boot patching
[ABK] Root command finished, but it returned no output.
[ABK] Root command failed, and it returned no output.
ABK does not currently have native management permission and cannot access this feature. Please use a kernel that recognizes ABK as the native manager.
Waiting for GitHub to return steps
%1$s waiting for GitHub to return steps
Unable to read module repository JSON: %1$s
Root node must be a JSON object
